subject: Statistics
title: Adaptive Randomization
description: A quick win: understand Adaptive Randomization.

hook.text: Halfway through a trial, one treatment is clearly better. Should you keep assigning patients to the worse one?

buildup.text: Adaptive randomization changes the assignment probability as data accumulates during the trial.

discovery.text: More patients get assigned to the treatment that's working better, while still maintaining a comparison group.

twist.text: Critics argue it complicates statistical analysis and can introduce bias if not handled carefully.

climax.text: Response-adaptive designs balance science and ethics: gathering evidence while helping more patients now.

punchline.text: Adapt as you learn. Ethics and evidence can work together.

quiz.question: What does adaptive randomization do during a clinical trial?
options: It shifts assignment probabilities so more patients receive the treatment that's performing better
It stops the trial immediately when one group is ahead
It removes the control group entirely
